title: Godetia decumbens

intro: Godetia decumbens, also known as the Indian paintbrush, is a flowering plant native to the western United States and Mexico, characterized by its bright red and yellow, brush-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Godetia decumbens include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
